name: Stripe Subscriptions
description: Data contract for the Stripe Subscriptions object (`/v1/subscriptions` API). The Subscription is the foundation of the SaaS revenue model — each row represents one customer's commitment to pay for a Product on a recurring schedule. This contract underpins the MRR / ARR calculations on the SaaS Revenue Metrics dashboard. Sourced via the Stripe REST API and webhook stream (`customer.subscription.*` events).

domain.name: Finance
domain.description: Revenue, billing, and customer financial relationships.

informationProduct.name: SaaS Revenue Metrics
informationProduct.description: MRR, ARR, ARPU, churn, expansion — the SaaS revenue dashboard.

dataAsset.name: raw.stripe.subscriptions
dataAsset.description: Snapshot of every Stripe Subscription with the full lifecycle history (status transitions, plan changes, cancellations). One row per subscription per change event.

team.name: Revenue Operations
team.description: Business owner — defines what counts as MRR vs. one-off revenue and signs off on the recognition logic.

team.name: Data Engineering
team.description: Owns the Stripe API ingestion job and webhook stream consumer.

team.name: Finance Analytics
team.description: Primary consumer — builds MRR / ARR / cohort / churn analyses on top of this contract.

team.name: Product Analytics
team.description: Secondary consumer — uses subscription data for retention and expansion modelling.

personas.name: Chief Revenue Officer (CRO)
personas.description: Owns the MRR / ARR numbers in the board pack — needs a contract this critical to be Production-grade.

personas.name: Chief Financial Officer (CFO)
personas.description: Signs off on the monthly revenue close that uses these subscriptions as the canonical recurring revenue list.

personas.name: Finance Analyst
personas.description: Builds and reconciles the MRR walk every month.

columns.name: id
columns.description: Stripe-issued unique subscription identifier. Always starts with `sub_` followed by a 14-character alphanumeric string.

columns.name: customer
columns.description: Foreign key — Stripe Customer ID this subscription belongs to. Joins to `raw.stripe.customers.id`.

columns.name: status
columns.description: Subscription lifecycle status: `trialing`, `active`, `past_due`, `unpaid`, `canceled`, `incomplete`, `incomplete_expired`, `paused`. Drives whether the subscription contributes to MRR (only `active`, `trialing`, `past_due`).

columns.name: plan_id
columns.description: Stripe Price ID the subscription is on. Joins to `raw.stripe.prices.id` to resolve the product, billing interval, and unit amount.

columns.name: quantity
columns.description: Number of seats / units the subscription covers. MRR contribution = price.unit_amount × quantity (normalised to monthly).

columns.name: currency
columns.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in lowercase. Inherited from the customer record.

columns.name: mrr_amount_local
columns.description: Monthly Recurring Revenue contribution in the subscription's local currency, in cents. For yearly subscriptions this is unit_amount / 12 × quantity. Calculated by the ingestion job — not a Stripe field.

columns.name: current_period_start
columns.description: Unix timestamp of the start of the current billing period.

columns.name: current_period_end
columns.description: Unix timestamp of the end of the current billing period — when the next invoice will be generated.

columns.name: cancel_at_period_end
columns.description: Boolean — `true` if the customer has scheduled cancellation at the end of the current period. A leading indicator of churn before the subscription transitions to `canceled`.

columns.name: canceled_at
columns.description: Unix timestamp the subscription was canceled (status → `canceled`). Null while active.

columns.name: trial_start
columns.description: Unix timestamp the trial period began. Null if the subscription was never in trial.

columns.name: trial_end
columns.description: Unix timestamp the trial period ended (or will end). Null if no trial.

columns.name: start_date
columns.description: Unix timestamp the subscription was first created. The acquisition date for cohort analysis.

columns.name: _extracted_at
columns.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when the row was extracted from Stripe.

glossaryTerms.name: Monthly Recurring Revenue (MRR)
glossaryTerms.description: The total contracted recurring revenue normalised to a monthly basis. Sum of mrr_amount_local across all subscriptions with status in (`active`, `trialing`, `past_due`), converted to USD at the contract's recognition rate.

glossaryTerms.name: Annual Recurring Revenue (ARR)
glossaryTerms.description: MRR × 12. The annualised run-rate of recurring revenue.

glossaryTerms.name: Churn
glossaryTerms.description: Subscriptions that transitioned from `active` / `trialing` / `past_due` to `canceled` in a given period. Customer churn = unique customers; revenue churn = MRR lost.

glossaryTerms.name: Expansion
glossaryTerms.description: Net increase in MRR from existing customers in a period (upgrades, additional seats, cross-sells), net of contractions (downgrades).

deliveryTypes.name: Google Cloud Storage (GCS)
deliveryTypes.description: Loaded into the `raw.stripe` schema in Snowflake every 1 hour for subscriptions (more frequent than customers because MRR drift is critical).

deliveryTypes.name: Stripe API
deliveryTypes.description: Source — REST API at https://api.stripe.com/v1/subscriptions plus webhook events.

trustRules.name: id non-null + Stripe format
trustRules.description: Every row must have a populated `id` matching the Stripe subscription ID format (`sub_` prefix).
trustRules.rule: id IS NOT NULL AND id LIKE 'sub_%'

trustRules.name: customer FK valid
trustRules.description: Every subscription's `customer` must resolve to a row in `raw.stripe.customers.id`. Orphans block MRR aggregation.

trustRules.name: status is a known Stripe value
trustRules.description: Status must be one of the documented Stripe subscription statuses. Unknown values would silently distort MRR.

trustRules.name: MRR amount non-negative
trustRules.description: MRR contribution can never be negative — credits are handled via invoice line items, not subscription MRR.

trustRules.name: Active subscriptions have non-zero MRR
trustRules.description: An active or trialing subscription with `mrr_amount_local = 0` indicates a calculation bug — investigate.

trustRules.name: Period start before period end
trustRules.description: current_period_start must be strictly less than current_period_end.

trustRules.name: Canceled subscriptions have canceled_at
trustRules.description: Subscriptions with status = `canceled` must have a populated canceled_at timestamp.
trustRules.rule: status != 'canceled' OR canceled_at IS NOT NULL

trustRules.name: Freshness within 90 minutes
trustRules.description: Latest `_extracted_at` across the dataset must be within the last 90 minutes — given the 1-hour cadence and webhook stream, anything older means the pipeline is degraded.

dataSyncs.name: Refresh frequency
dataSyncs.description: Bulk snapshot via the Stripe API every 1 hour; webhook stream applies incrementals continuously.

dataSyncs.name: Webhook event latency
dataSyncs.description: p95 latency from Stripe webhook event (`customer.subscription.updated`) to row appearing in Snowflake.

dataSyncs.name: Pipeline uptime
dataSyncs.description: Monthly availability target — this contract drives the board pack so the bar is high.

lineage.name: Stripe Billing Platform
lineage.description: The system of record for all subscriptions.

lineage.name: Custom Meltano Stripe Connector
lineage.description: Hourly pull from Stripe REST API into Snowflake `raw.stripe` schema.

lineage.name: MRR calculation job
lineage.description: Adds the calculated `mrr_amount_local` column by joining to `raw.stripe.prices` and normalising to monthly.
